title: Sideritis × murcica
taxonomy: Sideritis × murcica is a hybrid species of the genus Sideritis, commonly known as ironwort or Greek mountain tea. This plant is a cross between two species of Sideritis, specifically Sideritis romieuxii and Sideritis candica. The resulting hybrid is a unique and distinct species, which is often used in horticulture and as a medicinal plant. The classification of Sideritis × murcica reflects its complex origin and hybrid status. As a hybrid species, Sideritis × murcica is not assigned a specific rank in botanical classification, but is instead referred to by its hybrid name.
uses: Sideritis × murcica has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues. Its leaves, flowers, and stems are used in herbal remedies. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. It is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and colds. Additionally, Sideritis × murcica is believed to have a soothing effect on the digestive system.

intro: Sideritis × murcica, commonly known as the Murcian ironwort, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Sideritis, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Sideritis × murcica is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are utilized to treat various health conditions, including colds, flu, and gastrointestinal issues.
